title: Security Audit Agent Team (fail-closed gate + standalone audit)
description: Adds a single stack-aware bober-security-auditor role driving one runSecurityAudit() core, consumed by an in-pipeline fail-closed gate that vetoes sprint-passed on critical findings/timeout/parse-failure and a standalone `bober security-audit` CLI with a configurable blocking threshold. Findings reuse the locked ReviewResult shape, persist to .bober/security/, and flow into the priority hub; an opt-in deterministic scanner pre-filter (with slither and semgrep parsers) grounds the LLM judgment. Implements arch-20260712-security-audit-agent-team (ADR-1..6) for users whose generated code manages real customer funds.

clarificationQuestions.question: How deep should scanner pre-filter support go in this first build?

options.label: A
options.description: Generic command execution only; raw output as LLM priors

options.label: B
options.description: Include dedicated slither + semgrep JSON parsers

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — parsers as follow-up once real usage shows which scanners matter

clarificationQuestions.question: Should the standalone CLI get its own blocking threshold distinct from the gate's critical-only veto?
clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — simplest first cut; standaloneBlockOn stays the documented extension point

clarificationQuestions.question: Should findings surface beyond the .bober/security/ markdown in this build?

options.label: B
options.description: Also emit priority-hub Findings via the canonical FindingSchema

clarificationQuestions.question: Should the plan include a bober.security-audit skill wrapper, and should agent-bober dogfood the gate in its own bober.config.json?

options.label: A
options.description: CLI + skill, no dogfood config change

options.label: C
options.description: CLI + skill + enable security in agent-bober's own config

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A — matches the earlier dogfooding-declined precedent for budget

resolvedClarifications.answer: Include slither+semgrep parsers — ship dedicated JSON-output parsers mapping slither (Solidity) and semgrep (Node/TS) results to SecurityFinding[]; parsers are fixture-tested, binaries not required in CI.
Configurable threshold now — add security.standaloneBlockOn: 'critical' | 'important' (default 'critical'); affects ONLY the CLI exit code, never the pipeline gate veto (ADR-2 stays critical-only).

Also emit hub Findings — wire critical+important findings into the priority hub via the canonical FindingSchema imported from src/hub/finding.ts (never redefined), gated by security.hub (default true within the opt-in security section).
CLI + skill + dogfood — ship the bober.security-audit skill wrapper AND set security.enabled=true (LLM-only, no scanners) in agent-bober's own bober.config.json.

assumptions: The architecture document .bober/architecture/arch-20260712-security-audit-agent-team-architecture.md and ADR-1..6 are binding; sprints implement, not re-decide. All pipeline/schema anchors cited there (pipeline.ts:434-437/:466/:476-482, schema.ts:48-51/:74-88/:190, code-reviewer-agent.ts:17-37/:325-337, review-state.ts:12-15, eval-lenses.ts:7-8) were verified during the 5-checkpoint architecture flow.

Slither/semgrep parsers are pure functions tested against committed JSON output fixtures; scanner binaries are NOT required in CI (mirrors the MedlinePlus offline-fixtures convention in src/medical/). Live binaries run only when operators configure security.scanners.

Hub emission is local filesystem only (no egress) and default-on inside the already-opt-in security section: security.hub boolean default true, effective only when security.enabled=true. FindingSchema is imported from src/hub/finding.ts, never redefined (research-runner precedent, src/research/runner.ts).
standaloneBlockOn ('critical' | 'important', default 'critical') affects ONLY the bober security-audit exit code; the pipeline gate veto remains critical-only per ADR-2.

Dogfood config is LLM-only: security.enabled=true with scanners:[] in agent-bober's bober.config.json — avoids requiring slither/semgrep on dev machines; accepts ~1-5 min extra latency + LLM cost per sprint and the risk of false-positive blocks consuming retry iterations.
The auditor subagent runs with read-only tools; deterministic scanner execution stays in SecurityScannerPreFilter under the shared AbortSignal (architecture open question 5 resolved as assumed).
Stack→skill map covers bober.solidity/bober.anchor/bober.react now; all other stacks resolve to {skillName:null, generic taxonomy} — weaker coverage is the accepted ADR-1 residual risk.
outOfScope: Telegram warning surface for security findings (hub-only per Q3; Telegram is a follow-up spec)
Scanner parsers beyond slither + semgrep (no cargo-audit/mythril/npm-audit parsers; any binary still runs generically with raw-text priors)
Auto-reverting vulnerable auto-commits (per architecture risk table: retry regenerates; never fight the generator's commit)
Any mutation of code-reviewer/evaluator/documenter contracts or their advisory semantics (locked dependencies)
Auditing remote repositories (standalone target is a local path)
New egress axes (LLM calls use existing provider config; hub/store writes are local files)
Dedicated security skills for additional stacks (Move, Cairo, etc.)

features.title: Security config section, audit types, and .bober/security store
features.description: Opt-in default-off SecuritySectionSchema on BoberConfigSchema, SecurityAuditResult/SecurityFinding/VulnClass types wrapping the locked ReviewResult shape, and a security-audit-state store persisting markdown to .bober/security/.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When config.security is absent, BoberConfigSchema.parse output for every existing config fixture is deep-equal to the pre-change output (byte-identical default-off invariant).
AC2: SecurityAuditResult.verdict derives 'blocked' iff review.critical.length > 0, and 'pass' otherwise, via a pure exported function.

AC3: SecurityAuditStore round-trips: saveSecurityAudit writes .bober/security/<contractId>-security-audit.md, readSecurityAudit returns its content, listSecurityAudits lists saved ids; read of a missing id returns null without throwing.

features.title: Security auditor agent, runSecurityAudit core, and stack knowledge injection
features.description: The bober-security-auditor agent definition and the provider-agnostic runSecurityAudit() core (mirroring runCodeReviewer) with StackKnowledgeInjector mapping config.project.stack to skill checklists and the security-lens taxonomy.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When a mocked client returns a well-formed audit with a critical finding, runSecurityAudit returns SecurityAuditResult with that finding in review.critical, parsed:true, and verdict 'blocked'.
AC2: When the mocked client returns unparseable output, runSecurityAudit returns parsed:false and never a silent empty-critical 'pass' (inverts the code-reviewer's benign fallback).

AC3: StackKnowledgeInjector resolves solidity→bober.solidity, anchor→bober.anchor, react→bober.react, and unknown/absent stack → {skillName:null} with the generic VulnClass taxonomy; the resolved promptFragment appears in the auditor prompt.
AC4: All LLM interaction goes through the provider-agnostic createClient path; no provider SDK import appears outside src/providers/ adapter files.

features.title: Fail-closed pipeline security gate
features.description: SecurityAuditGate attached between the evaluation.passed check and the sprint-passed commit: time-boxed via Promise.race, blocking on critical finding/timeout/audit-error/parse-failure, feeding findings to the generator retry (ADR-5), skipping the documenter on block (ADR-6), byte-identical when disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When security.enabled=true and the audit returns a critical finding, the sprint is NOT marked passed, a security-audit-blocked history event is appended, and the next generator iteration receives the finding evidence in its feedback parts.
AC2: When the audit times out or throws (provider/budget/parse error), the gate blocks with reason 'timeout' or 'audit-error' — an incomplete audit is never treated as clean.
AC3: When config.security is absent, a paired pipeline run produces deep-equal results and identical history events to the pre-change pipeline (no gate code executes).
AC4: On a blocked round the documenter stage does not run; on a clean round the pipeline proceeds to sprint-passed, code-review, and documenter exactly as before.

features.title: Standalone security-audit CLI with configurable threshold
features.description: `bober security-audit [target]` running runSecurityAudit on demand with CI-friendly exit codes and a security.standaloneBlockOn threshold ('critical' | 'important', default 'critical').
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `bober security-audit` exits 2 when the configured threshold bucket is non-empty and 0 otherwise, printing a findings summary and the persisted artifact path.
AC2: With standaloneBlockOn:'important', important-bucket findings also produce exit 2; with the default 'critical', only critical findings do.
AC3: The pipeline gate's veto logic is unaffected by standaloneBlockOn (gate blocks on critical only, per ADR-2).

features.title: Scanner pre-filter with slither and semgrep parsers
features.description: SecurityScannerPreFilter running config.security.scanners under the shared audit AbortSignal with per-scanner isolation, plus dedicated slither-JSON and semgrep-JSON parsers producing SecurityFinding[] priors prepended to the auditor prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: The slither parser maps a committed slither JSON fixture to the expected SecurityFinding[] (path, line, severity, vulnClass); the semgrep parser does the same for a committed semgrep JSON fixture.
AC2: A missing scanner binary or nonzero exit yields [] for that scanner without throwing; other configured scanners still run.
AC3: When the shared AbortSignal fires, scanner child processes are SIGKILLed and the pre-filter resolves with partial findings.
AC4: Scanner findings appear as priors in the auditor prompt when scanners are configured; with scanners:[] the audit is a pure LLM pass with no child process spawned.

features.title: Priority-hub Findings emission
features.description: Critical and important audit findings emitted to the priority hub as canonical Findings (FindingSchema imported from src/hub/finding.ts), gated by security.hub (default true within the opt-in security section).
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When an audit completes with critical and/or important findings and security.hub is true, one hub Finding per audit finding is ingested via the existing finding-store ingest path with a severity mapping documented in code.
AC2: With security.hub:false, zero hub writes occur for an otherwise identical audit.
AC3: FindingSchema is imported from src/hub/finding.ts and never redefined; duplicate emission of the same finding is absorbed by the hub's existing content-hash dedup.

features.title: bober.security-audit skill, dogfood enable, and docs
features.description: A bober.security-audit skill mirroring skills/bober.code-review for Claude Code users, security.enabled=true (LLM-only) in agent-bober's own bober.config.json, and docs/security-audit.md.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: skills/bober.security-audit/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter and orchestrates the standalone audit (spawning the auditor / invoking the CLI) following the bober.code-review shape.
AC2: agent-bober's bober.config.json contains security.enabled=true with scanners:[] and parses cleanly under the extended schema.
AC3: docs/security-audit.md documents the config reference (every SecuritySectionSchema field), fail-closed gate semantics, CLI usage with exit codes, and the hub emission behavior.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Fail-closed invariants: unparseable auditor output, audit timeout, provider/budget error, and parse failure all produce a blocked verdict — never a silent pass.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit tests exercising each failure path assert blocked:true with the correct reason; the evaluator traces that no code path converts a failure into reason:'clean'.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Byte-identical behavior when config.security is absent: no new statement executes in the pipeline, and existing config fixtures parse to deep-equal outputs.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Paired-run deep-equal pipeline test plus config fixture snapshot tests (repo's established byte-identical convention).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The gate never hangs the pipeline: the audit resolves or is timed out within security.timeoutMs (default 300000 ms) via Promise.race, and scanner children die with the shared AbortSignal.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test with a never-resolving audit asserts the timeout branch returns blocked reason:'timeout' within the configured window (fake timers).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: TypeScript strict mode zero errors, ESLint flat config zero errors, collocated Vitest tests, ESM imports with .js extensions, no SDK types outside provider adapters.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm run build, npm test all pass; evaluator greps for SDK imports outside src/providers/.

integrationPoints: LLM provider via src/providers/ createClient (any of Anthropic/OpenAI-compat/Google/Ollama/claude-code)
Optional scanner binaries (slither, semgrep) via config.security.scanners
Priority hub finding-store (src/hub/) for Finding ingestion
techNotes.dataModel: SecurityAuditResult wraps the locked ReviewResult (critical[]/important[] buckets) adding stack, scannerRan, parsed, verdict; SecurityFinding extends ReviewFinding with optional VulnClass ('injection'|'authn-authz'|'secret-handling'|'input-validation'|'path-traversal'|'privilege-escalation'); SecuritySection config (enabled/failClosed/timeoutMs/model/maxTurns/provider/endpoint/providerConfig/budget/scanners/standaloneBlockOn/hub); two new history event kinds security-audit-clean and security-audit-blocked. Markdown artifacts under .bober/security/<contractId>-security-audit.md; verdict source of truth is in-memory + history events, never the markdown.
securityConsiderations: Fail-closed everywhere: parse failure, timeout, provider error, budget exhaustion all block (ADR-2)
Gate veto is critical-bucket-only; standaloneBlockOn affects the CLI only
Auditor subagent gets read-only tools; scanners run only in the pre-filter under the shared AbortSignal
Store write failures must never alter the already-computed verdict
No new egress: hub and store are local filesystem; LLM calls use existing provider config
techNotes.existingPatterns: Mirror src/orchestrator/code-reviewer-agent.ts (one core, pipeline attach + standalone entry, ReviewResult shape, Promise.race time-box at pipeline.ts:476-482); default-off Zod sections (evaluator panel, medical egress axes); src/state/review-state.ts for the store; src/research/runner.ts for canonical FindingSchema import + ingestFinding; src/cli/commands/research.ts for CLI command registration; MedlinePlus offline fixtures for parser tests; paired-run deep-equal byte-identity tests.

metadata.riskNotes: Sprint 3 touches the core pipeline.ts pass path — the only non-additive-module change; mitigated by the paired-run byte-identity test convention and the gate being unconstructed when disabled. All other sprints are additive modules. Dogfooding (sprint 7) accepts false-positive-block risk on this repo's own future sprints.
